title: Bolide Explosions
script_summary: An exploration into the science behind bolide explosions and their implications.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about bolide explosions than social media does.
A bolide is a space rock that explodes in our atmosphere, creating a fireball.
But not all bolides are the same. Some are tiny, while others could wipe out a city.
In 1908, the Tunguska event flattened 2,000 square kilometers in Siberia, likely from a small asteroid.
Science shows these explosions can teach us about planetary defense and the origins of our solar system.
Want to learn more about space? Hit that follow button and explore the universe with us!
